title: Scope GITHUB_TOKEN to least privilege
description: Declare an explicit permissions block defaulting to read and grant write only on the specific jobs that need it.
body: Use when authoring or reviewing any workflow, especially one that pushes commits, comments on PRs, or publishes packages. Do not leave the token at the repository default (often write-all) or grant packages:write on a job that only runs tests. Actions: add a top-level permissions: with contents: read, then override per-job with the narrowest scope (e.g. pull-requests: write only on the labeling job); inspect each step for what API calls it actually makes. Validation signals include a permissions block present at workflow or job level and a token that cannot push to protected branches. Risk boundary: widening GITHUB_TOKEN to write on a workflow triggered by untrusted input requires a human gate and a review of what the token can reach.
